response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates exceptional software craftsmanship, architectural thinking, and mentoring ability, he is not a suitable fit for this C++ systems engineering role. His last significant C++ work was 2014-2015 at National Instruments; the past decade has been entirely Node.js/TypeScript/web development, indicating a deliberate pivot away from systems programming. The role requires hands-on C++ development with STL, networking, multi-threading, and message brokers as core competencies, plus industrial automation and IEC 62443 security standards expertise—none of which are present in his background. Retraining a senior engineer to return to systems programming after a decade would be inefficient when candidates with current C++ expertise are available.
strengths: Exceptional software craftsmanship demonstrated through TDD, mentoring, Refactoring Dojos, and conference talks showing strong quality and leadership mindset
Proven ability to architect and design complex, scalable systems including microservices, event-driven platforms, and distributed applications across multiple domains
Diverse technical foundation with experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems showing adaptability and broad learning capability
concerns: C++ expertise severely outdated: last substantial C++ work ended 2014-2015; past decade entirely in Node.js/TypeScript/web development, indicating departure from systems programming
No industrial automation, machinery controls, or IoT background; experience is geospatial platforms and web services, not automation systems or embedded systems
Missing critical expertise: no evidence of SDL practices, IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards, MQTT protocol, or secure development lifecycle knowledge required for the role
